overview: A simple three-tier todo list application consisting of a React frontend (SPA) that communicates with a Go backend API, which persists data in PostgreSQL. The frontend sends HTTP requests to the backend service for CRUD operations on tasks, and the backend handles business logic and database interactions.
requirements: Users must be able to create new todo items with a text description
Users must be able to view all their todo items in a list
Users must be able to toggle todo items between complete and incomplete states
Users must be able to delete todo items
The UI must visually distinguish between completed and incomplete todos
The backend API must persist all todo data in PostgreSQL
All services must expose a /health endpoint for readiness checks
The frontend must be a single-page application built with React
The backend must provide RESTful API endpoints for todo CRUD operations

components.name: todo-api
components.openAPISpec:
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: Todo API
  version: 1.0.0
  description: RESTful API for managing todo items
paths:
  /health:
    get:
      summary: Health check endpoint
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Service is healthy
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  status:
                    type: string
                    example: ok
  /todos:
    get:
      summary: Get all todos
      responses:
        '200':
          description: List of all todos
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: array
                items:
                  $ref: '#/components/schemas/Todo'
    post:
      summary: Create a new todo
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              required:
                - text
              properties:
                text:
                  type: string
                  minLength: 1
                  maxLength: 500
      responses:
        '201':
          description: Todo created successfully
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/Todo'
        '400':
          description: Invalid input
  /todos/{id}:
    patch:
      summary: Update todo completion status
      parameters:
        - name: id
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: integer
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              required:
                - completed
              properties:
                completed:
                  type: boolean
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Todo updated successfully
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/Todo'
        '404':
          description: Todo not found
        '400':
          description: Invalid input
    delete:
      summary: Delete a todo
      parameters:
        - name: id
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: integer
      responses:
        '204':
          description: Todo deleted successfully
        '404':
          description: Todo not found
components:
  schemas:
    Todo:
      type: object
      properties:
        id:
          type: integer
          example: 1
        text:
          type: string
          example: Buy groceries
        completed:
          type: boolean
          example: false
        created_at:
          type: string
          format: date-time
          example: '2024-01-15T10:30:00Z'
      required:
        - id
        - text
        - completed
        - created_at
components.componentAgentInstructions: Implement a Go backend service using net/http that listens on port 9090. Use the pgx library to connect to PostgreSQL. Environment variables: DATABASE_URL (PostgreSQL connection string). Create a 'todos' table with columns: id (serial primary key), text (varchar 500), completed (boolean default false), created_at (timestamp default now()). Implement handlers for: GET /health (return {"status":"ok"}), GET /todos (return all todos as JSON array), POST /todos (accept {"text":"..."}, validate text is 1-500 chars, insert into DB, return created todo with 201), PATCH /todos/{id} (accept {"completed":true/false}, update the row, return updated todo or 404), DELETE /todos/{id} (delete the row, return 204 or 404). Use proper HTTP status codes. Enable CORS for all origins. Handle database connection errors gracefully. Log all requests. Use prepared statements to prevent SQL injection. On startup, ensure the todos table exists (create if missing). Return proper JSON error messages for 400/404 responses.

components.name: todo-web
components.openAPISpec:
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: Todo Web Application
  version: 1.0.0
  description: Frontend SPA for todo list management
paths:
  /:
    get:
      summary: Serve the React application
      responses:
        '200':
          description: HTML page with React app
          content:
            text/html:
              schema:
                type: string
  /health:
    get:
      summary: Health check endpoint
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Service is healthy
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  status:
                    type: string
                    example: ok
components.componentAgentInstructions: Create a React 18+ single-page application using TypeScript, Vite as the build tool, and a simple Node.js/Express server to serve the static files in production. The server should listen on port 8080 and serve the built React app from a 'dist' directory. Implement a /health endpoint that returns {"status":"ok"}. The React app should: use functional components with hooks, maintain todo state using useState, fetch todos from todo-api on mount via GET /todos, display todos in a list with checkboxes and delete buttons, show completed todos with strikethrough text styling, allow adding new todos via a text input and submit button (POST /todos with {"text":"..."}), toggle completion via checkbox clicks (PATCH /todos/{id} with {"completed":true/false}), delete todos via delete button clicks (DELETE /todos/{id}). Use fetch API for all HTTP requests. Read the todo-api base URL from environment variable REACT_APP_API_URL (default to http://localhost:9090 for development). Show loading states during API calls. Display error messages if API calls fail. Validate that todo text is not empty before submitting. Use CSS modules or styled-components for styling. Create a clean, responsive UI. The Docker container should run 'npm run build' to create production assets, then serve them with the Express server. Include a Dockerfile that uses multi-stage build: build stage with node:18 to run 'npm install' and 'npm run build', then runtime stage with node:18-slim to run the Express server.
